shuffle_note: Correct-answer line positions were spread across 0-3 by a seeded shuffle (topic-id hash). Post-shuffle constraint check passed: max_share <= 0.40 and all four positions used. $bias_defense_applied set true only after the shuffle and constraint check.

option_length_balance.note: Honest per-block check of whether the correct (=) option is the SOLE strict longest line in its answer block. longest_option_correct_count = 2 of 6 (q2 and q6). q2's correct option is an unavoidable obligation list; q6's is one of three comparably long lines. The correct answer is NOT systematically the longest: in q1, q3, q4, and q5 a distractor is the longest line. Distractors were lengthened in q1/q3/q4/q6 to keep options within rough parity.

questions.objective_ref: Recall, from 5.7, that the high-risk tier is allowed but tightly controlled, and explain why obligations attach to it.
Name the main obligations the EU AI Act places on high-risk systems, each in one plain sentence.
Distinguish a provider's duties from a deployer's duties at a plain level.
Explain what a conformity assessment is and why it must happen before a system reaches the market.
Give a concrete everyday example for each obligation (human oversight obligation).
Name the main obligations the EU AI Act places on high-risk systems (before-vs-ongoing ordering of the duties).
